name: reAlpha Tech Corp.

profile.description: reAlpha Tech Corp. is an AI-powered real estate technology company building an end-to-end homebuying platform integrating brokerage (Prevu), mortgage (reAlpha Mortgage, pending InstaMortgage), title (Hyperfast), and AI conversational services (Claire AI concierge). The company executed a 1-for-25 reverse stock split in April 2026 to regain Nasdaq compliance, announced a 25% workforce reduction in May 2026, and is restructuring to reduce cash burn. Founded in 2020, headquartered in Dublin, Ohio.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): FY2025 revenue grew 376% YoY to $4.5M with $116M in total transaction volume, but Q1 2026 revenue declined 9% to $841K, and the company remains deeply unprofitable with a $4.4M quarterly net loss [1]. The platform integration of Prevu, AiChat, and pending InstaMortgage shows aggressive assembly but no sustained commercial traction. AI Deployment (1/2): Claire AI concierge is a live, customer-facing AI product assisting homebuyers across 30 US states, and AiChat generates subscription revenue from its 250+ language conversational AI platform [2]. These are genuine AI products shipping to customers, but at minimal scale with unproven economics. Market Share (0/2): reAlpha has negligible market share in the $3T+ US real estate services market, competing against Zillow ($12B revenue), Redfin, and Rocket Mortgage with vastly greater resources [1][2]. Pipeline (1/2): InstaMortgage acquisition for ~$8.5M would add mortgage lending; Homebuying Hub launched February 2026; operations expanding across 30+ states [1]. General timelines but no specific revenue visibility. R&D Intensity (1/1): Heavy technology investment relative to revenue — 85+ employees (post-25% reduction) including Nepal-based development team, with AI/ML capabilities exceeding what $4.5M revenue would typically support [2].

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): reAlpha's mission to deliver 'a simpler, smarter, and more affordable path to homeownership' using AI is clear and connects to a meaningful social outcome (housing affordability), but it's not distinctive — numerous proptech companies make similar claims [2]. Community Impact (0/2): No evidence of charitable programs, community investment, or social impact beyond the core business promise [4]. Employee Retention (0/2): Company cut workforce 25% in May 2026, two board members resigned February 2026 creating audit committee non-compliance, and CEO transition occurred in June 2025 — organizational instability is evident [3]. Insider Buying (0/2): No evidence of meaningful insider buying. Multiple capital raises at steep discounts ($5M at $0.35/share pre-split in 2025) suggest management is issuing shares to fund survival rather than buying [3].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: LIQUIDITY FLOOR TRIGGERED: Post 1-for-25 reverse split (April 30, 2026), average daily volume is approximately 52,500 shares × $1.96 stock price = $102,900 daily dollar volume, well below the $1M liquidity minimum [5]. Even without the floor, financials are deeply distressed: Q1 2026 revenue of $841K with a $4.4M net loss, ROE of -271.5%, and going concern warning from management [1][3]. Cash declined from $7.8M to $4.7M in a single quarter (Q4 2025 to Q1 2026), providing roughly 3-4 quarters of runway at the current burn rate before additional dilutive financing is required. The 1-for-25 reverse split reduced shares from 134M to 5.36M, regaining Nasdaq minimum bid compliance as of May 2026, but the stock trades at $1.96 — still at risk of reversion below $1.00 [3]. Gross margin improved to 66% from 56%, the one positive metric [1].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (4/9): reAlpha's real estate brokerage and mortgage services face moderate AI disruption risk — traditional real estate agents and mortgage brokers are frequently cited as professions vulnerable to AI automation [2]. The company's strategy of building AI into its own platform (Claire AI, AiChat) attempts to be the disruptor, but this creates dependency on AI capabilities that well-funded competitors (Zillow, Redfin, Rocket Mortgage) could replicate with vastly greater resources. The title/escrow business (Hyperfast) has more regulatory protection from AI substitution. Transaction volume growth of 119% to $131.3M in Q1 2026 suggests the platform is functional, but at an insufficient scale to prove defensibility [1]. AI Opportunity (1/1): Claire AI and AiChat are live, customer-facing AI products generating revenue — Claire operates as 'real estate's first AI-powered, zero-commission smart buyer's agent' across 30 states, and AiChat's conversational AI platform supports 250+ languages [2].

infinite_game.summary: reAlpha is a 6-year-old startup fighting for survival rather than playing an infinite game. The company has pivoted from short-term rental investment to AI-powered homebuying, executed a 1-for-25 reverse stock split, cut 25% of its workforce, and faces going concern warnings — all within the last 12 months. Strategic decisions are driven by cash runway constraints and Nasdaq compliance deadlines, not an enduring purpose.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): reAlpha's stated purpose of making homeownership more affordable through AI connects to a genuine social need, but is indistinguishable from dozens of proptech companies [2]. The company pivoted from short-term rental investment (eAlpha Asset Management) to real estate technology to AI-powered homebuying in five years, suggesting the cause follows the opportunity rather than guiding strategy. The 25% workforce reduction and going concern warning indicate survival mode, not purpose-driven leadership [3].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): CEO Logozzo (BMW Financial Services) and Executive Chairman Devanur (scaled Ameri100 to $50M, E&Y Entrepreneur of the Year) bring credible institutional experience [2]. However, 25% workforce reduction in May 2026, two board resignations in February 2026 (creating audit committee non-compliance), CEO transition in June 2025, and rapid M&A integration create significant organizational stress [3]. The remaining ~85 employees face uncertainty about the company's survival.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): No evidence of reAlpha's leadership publicly acknowledging the strengths of Zillow, Redfin, Rocket Mortgage, or other dominant competitors. Company communications focus on Claire AI's differentiation as 'real estate's first' AI buyer's agent rather than learning from established players with billions in revenue [2].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): reAlpha pivoted from short-term rental asset management to real estate technology services, acquiring AiChat, Prevu, and pursuing InstaMortgage to build a vertically integrated platform [1][2]. The willingness to fundamentally reimagine the business model is evident. However, the pivots appear driven by financial necessity (original model wasn't scalable) and trend-chasing (AI hype) rather than a consistent Just Cause.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (0/2): The 25% workforce reduction, while painful, is a survival decision, not a courageous long-term investment [3]. Management's aggressive M&A growth (Prevu, AiChat, InstaMortgage) has been funded primarily through shareholder dilution at steep discounts ($5M at $0.35/share pre-split) rather than operational performance [3]. The terminated GTG Financial acquisition due to unmet payment terms raises execution questions. Leadership appears focused on reaching scale before running out of capital, not making principled long-term bets.

investment_thesis: reAlpha Tech Corp. is building an AI-powered end-to-end homebuying platform integrating brokerage, mortgage, title, and conversational AI across 30+ US states. FY2025 revenue surged 376% to $4.5M with $116M in total transaction volume, and Q1 2026 transaction volume grew 119% to $131.3M while gross margins expanded to 66% [1]. Claire AI operates as a zero-commission smart buyer's agent — a genuine AI product with differentiated positioning. The pending InstaMortgage acquisition would deepen vertical integration into mortgage lending. At ~$10.5M market cap, extreme pessimism is priced in, and the $3T+ US real estate services TAM provides massive headroom if the platform scales [1][2].
thesis_continued: The bear case is existential. Q1 2026 revenue declined 9% to $841K with a $4.4M net loss, cash fell from $7.8M to $4.7M in one quarter, and management issued a going concern warning [1][3]. The 1-for-25 reverse stock split in April 2026 reduced shares from 134M to 5.36M to regain Nasdaq compliance — but the stock at $1.96 remains at risk of reversion below $1.00. A 25% workforce reduction in May 2026 signals financial distress, and two board resignations in February created audit committee non-compliance [3]. Average daily dollar volume of ~$103K post-split is far below the $1M IOWN liquidity floor, making the stock effectively untradeable for institutional investors. The company competes against Zillow ($12B revenue), Redfin, and Rocket Mortgage with vastly greater resources. Serial dilution through capital raises has destroyed shareholder value. With no path to profitability visible and cash burn of ~$3M per quarter, additional dilutive financing is inevitable [1][3].
key_catalysts: InstaMortgage acquisition closing adds mortgage lending revenue and deepens the vertically integrated homebuying platform [1]
Claire AI and Homebuying Hub scaling across 30+ states, with Q1 2026 transaction volume growth of 119% to $131.3M demonstrating growing platform adoption [1]
Strategic restructuring (25% workforce reduction, $2M annualized savings) reducing cash burn and extending runway toward potential breakeven [3]
key_risks: Going concern risk — cash declined from $7.8M to $4.7M in Q1 2026 with ~$3M quarterly burn; management explicitly warns of doubt about continuing as a going concern [1][3]
Liquidity floor failure — ~$103K daily dollar volume post-reverse-split makes the stock effectively untradeable for institutional investors and IOWN [5]

Competition from Zillow, Redfin, and Rocket Mortgage with billion-dollar revenue bases dwarfs reAlpha's $4.5M annual revenue; Claire AI's zero-commission model has not proven profitable at any scale [2]
sources: reAlpha Q1 2026 Financial Results — $841K revenue (-9% YoY), $4.4M net loss, $131.3M transaction volume (+119%), going concern warning, May 2026
reAlpha Corporate Presentations — Claire AI platform, AiChat acquisition, team and product information, Homebuying Hub launch, 2025-2026
reAlpha SEC Filings and Nasdaq Compliance — 1-for-25 reverse split April 2026, 25% workforce reduction, board resignations, audit committee non-compliance, 2026
reAlpha FY2025 Annual Results — $4.5M revenue (+376% YoY), $116M transaction volume, Prevu/AiChat acquisitions, March 2026
Yahoo Finance / StockAnalysis — AIRE $1.96 stock price, ~$10.5M market cap, post-split volume data, accessed June 2026
